summary: Gruber recounts the extraordinary public exchange between Elon Musk and Halli Thorleifsson, a renowned Icelandic designer whose company Ueno was acquired by Twitter. After being locked out of Twitter's systems for nine days with no word on his employment status, Thorleifsson publicly asked Musk if he was still employed, prompting Musk to conduct a dismissive live interrogation on Twitter. Musk then mocked Thorleifsson's disability, claiming he did 'no actual work' — not knowing Thorleifsson has muscular dystrophy and was Iceland's Person of the Year. Gruber argues Thorleifsson masterfully baited Musk into publicly breaching an expensive contract, and highlights Thorleifsson's gracious yet devastating responses throughout. The saga ended with Musk issuing an apology.
key_points: Musk conducted what amounted to a public HR exit interview on Twitter, mocking a disabled employee whose contract made him expensive to fire
Thorleifsson is an award-winning designer, Iceland's Person of the Year, and philanthropist who voluntarily paid higher taxes on his company's acquisition
Musk likely didn't know what Figma was and mistook it for a 'ligma' joke, revealing his ignorance of basic design tools
Thorleifsson appears to have deliberately baited Musk into publicly breaching a contract that was on Twitter's 'do not fire' list due to its expense
Thorleifsson's closing joke about Musk's bathroom security fears was a masterful mic-drop moment
money_quotes: If you've been staying away from Twitter, you're smarter than me, but you might have missed this saga, and it is, I promise you, worth your attention.
As absurd and offensive as you might think their back-and-forth exchange was, I guarantee you're low-balling it.
Best guess as to why Musk responded thus is that Musk doesn't know what Figma is and thought Thorleifsson was making a 'ligma' joke.
The notion that a design leader can only 'work' while their hands are on a keyboard and trackpad/mouse is, of course, patently offensive.
Thorleifsson, to put it mildly, is a rather extraordinary and inspiring person.
The entire thread is best read as his having baited Musk, successfully, into breaching that contract in public.
Drop the mic, Halli, your work is done.
key_insight: Thorleifsson masterfully turned Musk's cruel public bullying of a disabled employee into a self-inflicted legal and reputational disaster for Musk, all while maintaining extraordinary grace.
